steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The phrase "a construction system known as ______" is a nonrestrictive appositive that renames "These temporary supports." The rule: a nonrestrictive appositive inserted into the middle of a sentence must be set off by paired commas (one before, one after).

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A (falsework;) — wrong. A semicolon joins two independent clauses; it cannot close a nonrestrictive appositive.
  • B (falsework) — wrong. Omitting punctuation leaves the appositive unclosed, so the comma that opened it has no partner.
  • C (falsework,) — correct. The comma after "falsework" pairs with the comma after "supports," properly enclosing the nonrestrictive appositive.
  • D (falsework:) — wrong. A colon introduces a list or explanation that follows an independent clause; it cannot close an appositive interrupting a sentence.
That matches choice C.
